blocking_issues.body:
[review] slice-status-plane-verifier-missing
Slice goal is STATUS_PLANE drift verifier coverage, but the only diff vs `main` is `controller/app.py` (account model/backoff logic).; No verifier/test artifact changes were made for `STATUS_PLANE.generated.yaml` readiness/deployment semantics.
Expected fix: Implement the requested STATUS_PLANE verifier coverage (and corresponding tests) that fails on drift from live readiness/deployment semantics.

[correctness] model-capability-fallback-regression
`effective_allowed_models_for_account` now returns `allowed_models` on no-overlap instead of capability-derived models.; Call sites (e.g. account/model selection and local review model selection) use that result directly and do not enforce capability membership afterward, so stale/configured `allowed_models` can select models not in live capability probes.
Expected fix: Reintroduce capability-safe fallback behavior (or explicit post-check against capability models) so selected models cannot bypass live capability constraints; add unit tests for overlap/no-overlap cases across auth kinds.

jury_feedback_history.summary: Current delta is off-slice and introduces untested account-model selection behavior that can drift from live capability semantics.

Reviewed the slice diff against `main` for regressions and missing tests; the new status-plane verifier is wired into Fleet verify, enforces readiness/deployment semantic parity from live admin status, and includes focused pass/fail/missing-artifact coverage with no actionable defects found.

blocking_issues.body:
[review] mirror-audit-row-not-bounded-to-stale-set
`scripts/ai/verify_design_mirror.py:108-122` only checks that `source_items` is non-empty, rooted under `/.codex-design/`, and free of `..`; it never checks that the queue row actually covers the stale mirror files it is supposed to bound.; Running `python3 scripts/ai/verify_design_mirror.py` on this branch returns `stale_paths=7 queue_errors=0`, with stale files including `.codex-design/product/README.md`, `VISION.md`, `USER_JOURNEYS.md`, and `RELEASE_PIPELINE.md`.; The published queue row at `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml:9-12` lists only `NEXT_90_DAY_PRODUCT_ADVANCE_REGISTRY.yaml`, `NEXT_90_DAY_QUEUE_STAGING.generated.yaml`, and `horizons/karma-forge.md`, so the verifier currently accepts a row that does not describe the full stale bundle.
Expected fix: Tighten the verifier so the single `audit-task-11707` row is validated against the actual stale mirror bundle it is standing in for, or regenerate the row so its bounded scope matches the stale files the verifier detects.

[tests] missing-stale-row-regression-tests
`Chummer.CoreEngine.Tests/Program.cs:5980-6045` only covers the clean-mirror/no-row case and the unexpected-leftover-file failure case.; There is no test that proves the intended new contract for this slice: stale mirror bytes plus exactly one bounded `audit-task-11707` queue row should be the only acceptable in-progress state.; There is also no test for the repeat-churn failure modes this change is meant to prevent, such as duplicate `audit-task-11707` rows or a malformed bounded row that the verifier should reject.
Expected fix: Add regression tests that exercise `verify_design_mirror.py` for the stale-with-single-row success path and for duplicate/misaligned queue-row failures.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The mirror-drift guard is close, but it does not actually enforce that the published `audit-task-11707` queue slice matches the stale mirror bundle, and the new verifier path is missing regression coverage for the intended stale-with-one-bounded-row behavior.

blocking_issues.body:
[contracts] queue-overlay-bounded-mirror-drift
`python3 scripts/verify_design_mirror_bundle.py --json` exits non-zero and reports `published_queue_overlay` with `status: queue_drift`, mismatches `mode:prepend` and `source_items`.; Current `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml` widens `source_items` to seven files (`NEXT_90_DAY_PRODUCT_ADVANCE_REGISTRY.yaml`, `PUBLIC_LANDING_POLICY.md`, `PUBLIC_DOWNLOADS_POLICY.md`, `PUBLIC_LANDING_MANIFEST.yaml`, `PUBLIC_FEATURE_REGISTRY.yaml`, `horizons/karma-forge.md`, plus queue staging) instead of the bounded two-file local mirror bundle.; The repo’s contract checks expect the overlay to stay bounded: `tests/test_design_mirror_bundle_contracts.py` and `tests/test_operator_contracts.py` both assert the mirror slice uses only `WEEKLY_PRODUCT_PULSE.generated.json` and `NEXT_90_DAY_QUEUE_STAGING.generated.yaml`, and `scripts/verify_design_mirror_bundle.py` enforces the same shape.
Expected fix: Restore `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml` to the bounded mirror-drift contract expected by the verifier and tests: keep the single `audit-task-4257456` slice, set the queue mode to the repo’s expected mode, and reduce `source_items` back to the two local design-mirror bundle files.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The reviewed slice currently violates its own bounded design-mirror queue contract: `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml` has drifted to `mode: prepend` with widened `source_items`, so the repo’s mirror-bundle verifier fails even though the mirrored product files themselves are in parity.

[review] slice-scope-drift-mixed-branch
`git diff --name-only main...HEAD` spans 154 files across `.codex-design/`, `.codex-studio/`, `docs/`, `scripts/`, `src/`, `tests/`, `feedback/`, and `logs/`, so this is not a bounded single-slice review surface.; The prompt is internally inconsistent: `Current slice` names the Dense/U4-U5 queue item, while the compact review packet title names the Catalog/U7-U8 queue item.; Representative non-slice files in the diff include adapter code, token canon changes, preview/catalog changes, verify script changes, test harness changes, and many snapshot fixtures, so visual/accessibility/test findings cannot be attributed safely to one authoritative slice.; This exact blocker is already recorded in `feedback/2026-04-21-github-review-pr.md` and `feedback/2026-04-22-github-review-pr.md`, and current branch state still matches that mixed-branch condition.; `AGENTS.md` requires `Keep changes scoped to the queue item.`
Expected fix: Restack or split the branch so it contains only the authoritative queue slice under review, or explicitly widen and reconcile the slice definition and review packet so the changed files match that scope before re-requesting review.

blocking_issues.body:
[contracts] contracts-quarantine-still-exports-upstream-dtos
`MediaFactoryContracts.cs` still publicly exports upstream-authoring DTOs such as `PacketFactoryRequest`, `PacketAttachment*`, and `RouteCinema*` with fields like `Subject`, `Attachments`, `WaypointScript`, `TravelSummary`, and `ReviewState` ([lines 149-223]).; The guardrail script now accepts those mixed DTOs as long as they carry an `EXTRACT-008A quarantine` obsolete marker instead of failing on their continued presence ([scripts/ai/contract-boundary-tests.sh:68-90]).; This still violates the repo review rule to keep DTOs render-only rather than merely quarantining upstream semantics inside the public media contract package.
Expected fix: Remove these mixed compatibility DTOs from the public `Chummer.Media.Contracts` surface or move them behind the upstream owner seam, and make the boundary test fail on their presence rather than permitting quarantine markers.

[tests] runtime-verify-missing-reject-lifecycle-coverage
The verifier exercises approval, pin/persist, backup/restore, cache expiry, and job expiry, but it never drives an approval-gated asset through `Rejected` state ([Program.cs:7-108]).; `AGENTS.md` and `.codex-design/review/REVIEW_CONTEXT.md` require lifecycle coverage for approval/persist/reject, yet the executable regression path only proves the approve/persist branch.; Because `scripts/ai/verify.sh` relies on this runtime verifier, the reject path can regress without any executable signal.
Expected fix: Add a reject-path verification case that rejects an approval-gated asset and asserts rejected retention state, rejection timestamp behavior, no persisted/pinned carryover, and correct restore behavior after backup/restore.

[review] milestone-m8-completion-truth-still-overstates-dto-closure
HEAD marks `M8 finished media plant` as `complete` based on `DS-01`..`DS-05` alone ([IMPLEMENTATION_SCOPE.md lines 120-127 in HEAD]).; The DTO execution evidence explicitly says the generic DTO prompt is not fully closed by `DS-01`..`DS-05`, inventories remaining compatibility residue, and maps follow-on work to `DS-06`..`DS-09` ([docs/EXTRACT-008-DS-execution-evidence.md:239-305]).; `WORKLIST.md` simultaneously keeps `EXTRACT-008A` queued because the compatibility DTO residue is still open ([WORKLIST.md:29-30]).
Expected fix: Update milestone coverage/completion truth so M8 and the DTO-boundary lane reflect the still-open `EXTRACT-008A` compatibility residue instead of claiming full closure from `DS-01`..`DS-05`.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The branch still carries the previously reported render-boundary leakage and lifecycle/test gaps: compatibility DTOs remain publicly exported under quarantine markers, reject-path lifecycle coverage is still missing from runtime verification, and milestone truth still overstates M8/DTO closure despite the queued EXTRACT-008A residue.

[review] slice-status-plane-verifier-missing
Slice goal is STATUS_PLANE drift verifier coverage, but the only diff vs `main` is `controller/app.py` (account model/backoff logic).; No verifier/test artifact changes were made for `STATUS_PLANE.generated.yaml` readiness/deployment semantics.
Expected fix: Implement the requested STATUS_PLANE verifier coverage (and corresponding tests) that fails on drift from live readiness/deployment semantics.

[correctness] model-capability-fallback-regression
`effective_allowed_models_for_account` now returns `allowed_models` on no-overlap instead of capability-derived models.; Call sites (e.g. account/model selection and local review model selection) use that result directly and do not enforce capability membership afterward, so stale/configured `allowed_models` can select models not in live capability probes.
Expected fix: Reintroduce capability-safe fallback behavior (or explicit post-check against capability models) so selected models cannot bypass live capability constraints; add unit tests for overlap/no-overlap cases across auth kinds.

jury_feedback_history.summary: Current delta is off-slice and introduces untested account-model selection behavior that can drift from live capability semantics.

Reviewed the slice diff against `main` for regressions and missing tests; the new status-plane verifier is wired into Fleet verify, enforces readiness/deployment semantic parity from live admin status, and includes focused pass/fail/missing-artifact coverage with no actionable defects found.

blocking_issues.body:
[review] mirror-audit-row-not-bounded-to-stale-set
`scripts/ai/verify_design_mirror.py:108-122` only checks that `source_items` is non-empty, rooted under `/.codex-design/`, and free of `..`; it never checks that the queue row actually covers the stale mirror files it is supposed to bound.; Running `python3 scripts/ai/verify_design_mirror.py` on this branch returns `stale_paths=7 queue_errors=0`, with stale files including `.codex-design/product/README.md`, `VISION.md`, `USER_JOURNEYS.md`, and `RELEASE_PIPELINE.md`.; The published queue row at `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml:9-12` lists only `NEXT_90_DAY_PRODUCT_ADVANCE_REGISTRY.yaml`, `NEXT_90_DAY_QUEUE_STAGING.generated.yaml`, and `horizons/karma-forge.md`, so the verifier currently accepts a row that does not describe the full stale bundle.
Expected fix: Tighten the verifier so the single `audit-task-11707` row is validated against the actual stale mirror bundle it is standing in for, or regenerate the row so its bounded scope matches the stale files the verifier detects.

[tests] missing-stale-row-regression-tests
`Chummer.CoreEngine.Tests/Program.cs:5980-6045` only covers the clean-mirror/no-row case and the unexpected-leftover-file failure case.; There is no test that proves the intended new contract for this slice: stale mirror bytes plus exactly one bounded `audit-task-11707` queue row should be the only acceptable in-progress state.; There is also no test for the repeat-churn failure modes this change is meant to prevent, such as duplicate `audit-task-11707` rows or a malformed bounded row that the verifier should reject.
Expected fix: Add regression tests that exercise `verify_design_mirror.py` for the stale-with-single-row success path and for duplicate/misaligned queue-row failures.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The mirror-drift guard is close, but it does not actually enforce that the published `audit-task-11707` queue slice matches the stale mirror bundle, and the new verifier path is missing regression coverage for the intended stale-with-one-bounded-row behavior.

[contracts] queue-overlay-bounded-mirror-drift
`python3 scripts/verify_design_mirror_bundle.py --json` exits non-zero and reports `published_queue_overlay` with `status: queue_drift`, mismatches `mode:prepend` and `source_items`.; Current `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml` widens `source_items` to seven files (`NEXT_90_DAY_PRODUCT_ADVANCE_REGISTRY.yaml`, `PUBLIC_LANDING_POLICY.md`, `PUBLIC_DOWNLOADS_POLICY.md`, `PUBLIC_LANDING_MANIFEST.yaml`, `PUBLIC_FEATURE_REGISTRY.yaml`, `horizons/karma-forge.md`, plus queue staging) instead of the bounded two-file local mirror bundle.; The repo’s contract checks expect the overlay to stay bounded: `tests/test_design_mirror_bundle_contracts.py` and `tests/test_operator_contracts.py` both assert the mirror slice uses only `WEEKLY_PRODUCT_PULSE.generated.json` and `NEXT_90_DAY_QUEUE_STAGING.generated.yaml`, and `scripts/verify_design_mirror_bundle.py` enforces the same shape.
Expected fix: Restore `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml` to the bounded mirror-drift contract expected by the verifier and tests: keep the single `audit-task-4257456` slice, set the queue mode to the repo’s expected mode, and reduce `source_items` back to the two local design-mirror bundle files.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The reviewed slice currently violates its own bounded design-mirror queue contract: `.codex-studio/published/QUEUE.generated.yaml` has drifted to `mode: prepend` with widened `source_items`, so the repo’s mirror-bundle verifier fails even though the mirrored product files themselves are in parity.

`MediaFactoryContracts.cs` still publicly exports upstream-authoring DTOs such as `PacketFactoryRequest`, `PacketAttachment*`, and `RouteCinema*` with fields like `Subject`, `Attachments`, `WaypointScript`, `TravelSummary`, and `ReviewState` ([lines 149-223]).; The guardrail script now accepts those mixed DTOs as long as they carry an `EXTRACT-008A quarantine` obsolete marker instead of failing on their continued presence ([scripts/ai/contract-boundary-tests.sh:68-90]).; This still violates the repo review rule to keep DTOs render-only rather than merely quarantining upstream semantics inside the public media contract package.
Expected fix: Remove these mixed compatibility DTOs from the public `Chummer.Media.Contracts` surface or move them behind the upstream owner seam, and make the boundary test fail on their presence rather than permitting quarantine markers.

[tests] runtime-verify-missing-reject-lifecycle-coverage
The verifier exercises approval, pin/persist, backup/restore, cache expiry, and job expiry, but it never drives an approval-gated asset through `Rejected` state ([Program.cs:7-108]).; `AGENTS.md` and `.codex-design/review/REVIEW_CONTEXT.md` require lifecycle coverage for approval/persist/reject, yet the executable regression path only proves the approve/persist branch.; Because `scripts/ai/verify.sh` relies on this runtime verifier, the reject path can regress without any executable signal.
Expected fix: Add a reject-path verification case that rejects an approval-gated asset and asserts rejected retention state, rejection timestamp behavior, no persisted/pinned carryover, and correct restore behavior after backup/restore.

[review] milestone-m8-completion-truth-still-overstates-dto-closure
HEAD marks `M8 finished media plant` as `complete` based on `DS-01`..`DS-05` alone ([IMPLEMENTATION_SCOPE.md lines 120-127 in HEAD]).; The DTO execution evidence explicitly says the generic DTO prompt is not fully closed by `DS-01`..`DS-05`, inventories remaining compatibility residue, and maps follow-on work to `DS-06`..`DS-09` ([docs/EXTRACT-008-DS-execution-evidence.md:239-305]).; `WORKLIST.md` simultaneously keeps `EXTRACT-008A` queued because the compatibility DTO residue is still open ([WORKLIST.md:29-30]).
Expected fix: Update milestone coverage/completion truth so M8 and the DTO-boundary lane reflect the still-open `EXTRACT-008A` compatibility residue instead of claiming full closure from `DS-01`..`DS-05`.

jury_feedback_history.summary: The branch still carries the previously reported render-boundary leakage and lifecycle/test gaps: compatibility DTOs remain publicly exported under quarantine markers, reject-path lifecycle coverage is still missing from runtime verification, and milestone truth still overstates M8/DTO closure despite the queued EXTRACT-008A residue.
